  1. Home

  3. › Raw Food (BARF)

Raw Food (BARF) Diet for Dogs

Raw food diets, often called BARF (Biologically Appropriate Raw Food), consist of uncooked meat, bones, organs, and vegetables. Proponents argue it mimics a dog's ancestral diet.

✓ Benefits

  • Improved coat and skin condition
  • Smaller and firmer stools
  • Higher moisture content
  • Potential dental benefits from raw bones

⚠ Risks & Considerations

  • Risk of bacterial contamination (Salmonella, Listeria)
  • Nutritional imbalance if not properly formulated
  • Potential for bone splintering
  • Higher cost and preparation time

Who Is This Diet Suitable For?

healthy adult dogs working breeds owners committed to food safety practices

Feeding Guidelines by Dog Size

These are general guidelines for the Raw Food (BARF) diet. Always adjust portions based on your dog's individual weight, activity level, and life stage. Consult your vet before making significant changes to your dog's diet.

Size Weight Range Cups Per Day Feeding Frequency
Small up to 10 kg 2-4% of body weight in oz cups 2 meals
Medium 10–25 kg 2-3% of body weight in oz cups 2 meals
Large 25–45 kg 2-3% of body weight in oz cups 2 meals
Giant 45 kg+ 1.5-2% of body weight in oz cups 2-3 meals

Cups are a rough guide based on standard 8 fl oz measuring cups. Always follow the manufacturer's label for calorie-dense or prescription formulas.

Recommended Brands

The following brands are well-regarded within the raw food (barf) category. Look for products where a named protein source appears as the first ingredient.

  • Darwin's Natural Pet Products
  • Primal Pet Foods
  • Nature's Variety Instinct Raw
  • Steve's Real Food

Top Picks: Raw Food (BARF) Dog Food

A leading raw food (barf) formula with high-quality protein as the first ingredient. Suitable for adult dogs across all sizes. Improved coat and skin condition.

Supplement

Digestive Support Supplement

Probiotics and digestive enzymes to complement a raw food (barf) diet. Helps maintain gut balance, especially during dietary transitions. Helps offset: risk of bacterial contamination (salmonella, listeria).
